2606.14674.title: AgentSpec: Understanding Embodied Agent Scaffolds Through Controlled Composition
2606.14674.paper_summary: LLM agents are increasingly built not as single model calls, but as scaffolded systems that combine reasoning, memory, reflection, action execution, and learning. While such scaffolds often improve performance, they are often embedded in tightly coupled pipelines, making it difficult to isolate component contributions, compare alternative designs, or understand how module interactions shape agent behavior. We introduce AgentSpec, a modular specification framework that represents embodied agents as typed compositions of reusable policy components with standardized interfaces. AgentSpec standardizes the interfaces among perception, memory, reasoning, reflection, action, and optional learning, enabling components to be swapped and recombined under controlled conditions. We instantiate this framework across DeliveryBench, ALFRED, MiniGrid, and RoboTHOR, and analyze reasoning, memory, reflection, and reinforcement-learning modules across model backbones. Our results show that agent performance is governed by scaffold compatibility and interaction effects rather than isolated module strength. In particular, structured multi-granularity memory improves long-horizon state tracking, reasoning and memory interact non-uniformly across environments, reflection trades off correction and cost, and RL-trained policies compose best when optimized with deployment-time scaffold structure. AgentSpec provides a controlled foundation for studying, comparing, and designing composable LLM agents. Our code, baselines and interactive playground are publicly available at https://agentspec-embodied.github.io.
2606.14674.paper_summary_zh: LLM 代理越來越多地不是作為單一模型調用來構建，而是作為結合推理、記憶、反思、行動執行和學習的支架系統。<lb/>雖然這些支架通常能提高性能，但它們往往嵌入在緊密耦合的管道中，使得難以隔離組件貢獻、比較替代設計或理解模塊交互如何塑造代理行為。<lb/>我們引入了 AgentSpec，一個模塊化的規範框架，將具身代理表示為具有標準化接口的可重用策略組件的類型組合。<lb/>AgentSpec 標準化了感知、記憶、推理、反思、行動和可選學習之間的接口，使得組件可以在受控條件下進行替換和重新組合。<lb/>我們在 DeliveryBench、ALFRED、MiniGrid 和 RoboTHOR 中實現了這一框架，並分析了模型主幹中的推理、記憶、反思和強化學習模塊。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，代理性能受支架兼容性和交互效應的影響，而不是孤立模塊的強度。<lb/>特別是，結構化的多粒度記憶改善了長期狀態跟踪，推理和記憶在不同環境中非均勻地互動，反思在修正和成本之間進行權衡，而經過強化學習訓練的策略在優化部署時的支架結構時表現最佳。<lb/>AgentSpec 為研究、比較和設計可組合的 LLM 代理提供了一個受控的基礎。我們的代碼、基準和互動遊樂場可在 https://agentspec-embodied.github.io 上公開獲得。
2606.14674.author: Jixuan Chen et.al.
2606.14674.authors: Jixuan Chen, Jianzhi Shen, Haoqiang Kang, Zhi Hong, Qingyi Jiang, Soham Bose, Yiming Zhang, Leon Leng, Amit Vyas, Lingjun Mao, Siru Ouyang, Kun Zhou, Lianhui Qin
